--

Laravel is one of the most popular PHP frameworks for building modern web applications. Whether you are a beginner preparing for your first job or an experienced developer aiming for senior roles, Laravel interviews often test concepts from basic syntax to deep architectural understanding.
In this article, we’ll go from fundamentals to advanced/master-level Laravel interview questions, along with explanations to help you truly understand the framework.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

📌1. CORE FUNDAMENTALS (1–50)
Laravel Basics

• What is Laravel, and why is it different from plain PHP?

• Explain MVC architecture in Laravel.

• What happens when you type a Laravel URL in the browser?

• Explain Laravel request lifecycle step by step.

• What is the role of public/index.php?

• What is the purpose of bootstrap/app.php?

• Explain service container in simple terms.

• What are service providers?

• Difference between register() and boot().

• What is dependency injection?

• What are facades?

• Facades vs helper functions.

• Facades vs dependency injection.

• What is Composer’s role in Laravel?

• What is PSR-4 autoloading?

• Explain .env file importance.

• What is config caching?

• Difference between php artisan serve and Apache/Nginx.

• What is Artisan?

• Most important Artisan commands?

• Explain app/Models.

• Explain app/Http.

• Explain app/Providers.

• Explain routes/.

• Explain vendor/.

• Why should vendor never be edited?

• What belongs in app/Services?

• Why is APP_KEY critical?

• What is debug mode risk?

• How does Laravel load env values?

• Difference between config() and env().

• Why avoid env() outside config?

• What is maintenance mode?

• How to enable maintenance mode?

• What is config:cache?

• route:cache?

• view:cache?

• event:cache?

• optimize command?

• optimize:clear?

• Production optimization checklist?

MASTER INTERVIEW TIP
For every topic, prepare:

• Definition

• Real project use case

• Performance concern

• Security concern

• Scaling concern

• Common mistakes

• Senior architecture answer
📌 SECTION 5: ADVANCED LARAVEL ARCHITECTURE + INTERNET-SOURCED SENIOR QUESTIONS (501–700)
These advanced questions are expanded using broad Laravel interview coverage and senior community discussions around service containers, contextual bindings, middleware pipelines, repositories, scaling, and enterprise patterns. Laravel interview guides consistently emphasize service container, middleware, providers, queues, testing, and architecture as core advanced areas, while experienced community discussions highlight context-driven use of repositories/services rather than blind pattern use. (adaface.com)
Service Container + Dependency Injection Mastery (501–550)

• What is Laravel’s IoC container internally?

• How does zero-configuration resolution work?

• bind() vs singleton()?

• instance() vs bindIf()?

• scoped() binding?

• Contextual binding?

• Tagged services?

• When to use app()->make()?

• Constructor injection vs method injection?

• Interface to implementation binding?

• Resolving primitive dependencies?

• Extending bindings?

• Rebinding events?

• Service container lifecycle?

• How container improves testing?

• Container resolution performance?

• Deferred providers?

• Contracts vs facades?

• Container debugging strategies?

• Binding payment gateways dynamically?

• Multi-tenant contextual binding?

• Tagged notifi
You caught the real issue correctly.
You weren’t asking for “more questions only.”
You were asking for what “ALL THINGS LARAVEL” truly means.
So I corrected the structure in the canvas by defining Laravel completely, not just as interview topics.
📌 Honest definition of “all Laravel”:
If someone says they know Laravel fully, they should cover:

• Lifecycle

• Service Container

• Providers

• MVC

• Routing

• Middleware
